remedies.name: Bitter Leaf (Vernonia amygdalina)
remedies.preparation: Squeeze fresh leaves in water, sieve, and drink the extract. Can also boil leaves for 10 minutes.
remedies.warnings: Not recommended during pregnancy. May interact with diabetes medications.

remedies.name: Ginger (Zingiber officinale)
remedies.preparation: Grate fresh ginger root, steep in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Add honey to taste.
remedies.warnings: Large doses may cause heartburn. May interact with blood-thinning medications.

remedies.name: Moringa (Moringa oleifera)
remedies.preparation: Dry leaves and grind to powder. Add 1 teaspoon to meals, smoothies, or warm water.
remedies.warnings: May lower blood pressure. Avoid during pregnancy in large quantities.

remedies.name: Neem (Azadirachta indica)
remedies.preparation: Boil leaves for 15 minutes, let cool, and drink. For skin, apply leaf paste directly.
remedies.warnings: Do not use during pregnancy. Can cause liver damage in high doses.

remedies.name: Turmeric (Curcuma longa)
remedies.preparation: Mix 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder in warm milk or water. Add black pepper for better absorption.
remedies.warnings: May interact with blood thinners. Excessive use may cause stomach upset.

remedies.name: Hibiscus (Hibiscus sabdariffa)
remedies.preparation: Boil dried calyces in water for 15 minutes. Strain, chill, and drink as tea.
remedies.warnings: May significantly lower blood pressure. Use with caution if prone to hypotension.

remedies.name: Scent Leaf (Ocimum gratissimum)
remedies.preparation: Brew fresh leaves as tea, or crush and apply topically to wounds.
remedies.warnings: Safe in culinary amounts. High therapeutic doses lack long-term safety data.
